text.title: requirements-validation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for requirements-validation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using walkthrough for all requirement types regardless of complexity or domain.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Walkthrough is collaborative but informal; it systematically misses performance defects, usability problems, and process gaps that other techniques expose.

failure-mode.description: Rubber-stamp approval: stakeholders sign off without reading or without the authority to approve.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A sign-off from someone without decision rights is political theatre; when requirements prove wrong, the signer has no accountability and the BA has no defensible baseline.

failure-mode.description: Missing key stakeholders: validation session held without users, domain SMEs, or technical leads.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each missing stakeholder type leaves a class of defects undetected — users find usability gaps, SMEs find domain errors, tech leads find feasibility issues.

failure-mode.description: Reviewing 200 requirements in one session without splitting by theme or priority.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Attention exhausts by requirement 30; the remaining 170 receive rubber-stamp treatment regardless of intent.

failure-mode.description: Confusing validation with UAT: validating requirements after delivery rather than before build.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: UAT validates the built solution; requirements validation validates the requirement before the build. Different artefact, different audience, different defect cost — at UAT it costs a sprint; at requirements validation it costs a session.

failure-mode.description: Value_class=none requirement left in the validated set without removal or re-elicitation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A requirement that traces to no business value is gold-plating; implementing it wastes delivery capacity and may create technical debt with no benefit offset.

failure-mode.description: LLM runs a verification checklist (well-formed, unambiguous, testable) and reports "validation complete."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Validation specifically asks "does this requirement deliver value?" — the value question is absent from verification; an LLM that conflates them produces a false green signal.

failure-mode.description: LLM invents stakeholder confirmations ("Product Owner confirmed requirement is valid") without an evidence anchor.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hallucinated approvals have no accountability chain; when requirements prove wrong, the invented confirmation cannot be traced to any human decision.

failure-mode.description: LLM proposes new requirements during validation ("we should also consider X").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Scope expansion during validation returns the process to elicitation; mixing the two phases corrupts both the baseline and the validation record.

failure-mode.description: Validation-verification conflation. LLMs default to "is this well-written?" instead of "does this deliver value?"
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force the value question: every prompt names the Business Need.

failure-mode.description: Hallucinated stakeholders. Models invent SME confirmations.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require an evidence anchor (chat permalink, ticket, signed PDF hash) before any "approved" status.

failure-mode.description: Confirmation bias. When asked "are these OK?", LLMs trend toward yes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use adversarial framing: "list 3 ways each requirement could fail to deliver value" before scoring.

failure-mode.description: Severity inflation. Agents tag everything High to look thorough.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Anchor severity to the rule above and reject ungrounded H ratings.

failure-mode.description: Lost provenance. Agents quote the requirement back to itself instead of the elicitation/business-need source.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: evidence_quote must come from upstream sources.

failure-mode.description: Scope drift. Agents propose new requirements during validation — that returns to elicitation, not validation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Explicitly scope the agent's role: classify existing requirements only, no new proposals.

failure-mode.description: Stakeholder simulation. Role-playing "as Product Owner" is a dry run, not a sign-off.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: Token cost on large specs. Loading the whole spec per requirement explodes cost.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cache the Business Need / Goals; batch validation by section.

failure-mode.description: Locale plus a11y blind spots. Agents pass i18n/a11y requirements silently.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add a per-validation locale plus a11y matrix.
